model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DEPTH = 2","new":"DEPTH = 3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Increase MLP capacity (one extra hidden layer and wider base width) to improve representational power within the fixed 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0","new":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.1","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Add moderate regularization to the larger MLP to reduce overfitting and improve validation loss under the short fixed-step budget.","secondary_modes":["layout","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.1","new":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The previous regularization increase regressed validation loss, so revert to the stronger prior setting before exploring new directions.","secondary_modes":["layout","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With the larger MLP and warmup+cosine schedule, a moderately higher peak learning rate may improve progress within the fixed 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["summaries","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Restore the better learning rate and test a larger batch to improve gradient stability and sample coverage per optimization step under the fixed-step evaluator.","secondary_modes":["topk","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The strong gain from increasing batch size suggests this fixed-step setup benefits from more examples per update; test 256 for further improvement.","secondary_modes":["topk","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Batch-size scaling has produced the largest gains so far; test 512 to further increase examples seen per fixed optimization step.","secondary_modes":["topk","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With batch size now at 512, a higher learning rate is more likely to be stable and can improve optimization speed within 256 steps.","secondary_modes":["micro","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Now that optimization is stronger (large batch + higher LR), modestly increasing hidden width may improve model capacity without adding architectural complexity.","secondary_modes":["micro","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"        return max(0.05, cosine)","new":"        return max(0.01, cosine)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"Allow a lower final learning-rate floor during cosine decay so late-stage updates can refine more at the end of the fixed 256-step run.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 0.0","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"        return max(0.01, cosine)","new":"        return max(0.05, cosine)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Revert the scheduler-floor regression and test removing weight decay, since very large-batch training with batchnorm may benefit from less explicit L2 regularization in this short-step regime.","secondary_modes":["summaries","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 1024","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Scaling batch size has been consistently beneficial; test 1024 to maximize sample throughput per fixed training step.","secondary_modes":["topk","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 1024","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 2048","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Given the strong monotonic gains from larger batches, test 2048 to further increase data coverage per fixed optimization step.","secondary_modes":["topk","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After strong gains from very large batches, increase learning rate to better match the lower-gradient-noise regime and improve progress within the fixed step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 2048","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 4096","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Continue the successful large-batch scaling trend by testing 4096 examples per step for even greater data coverage under fixed-step evaluation.","secondary_modes":["topk","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WARMUP_EPOCHS = 2","new":"WARMUP_EPOCHS = 1","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"With very large batches, shortening warmup should let the run reach high learning rates sooner and use more of the 256-step budget for effective optimization.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WARMUP_EPOCHS = 1","new":"WARMUP_EPOCHS = 2","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.98)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"Restore the better warmup setting and test a lower AdamW second-moment beta, which can improve adaptation speed in very-large-batch training.","secondary_modes":["summaries","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After improving optimizer dynamics with lower beta2, test a slightly higher peak learning rate to speed optimization in the large-batch regime.","secondary_modes":["indexing","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 4096","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 8192","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Large-batch scaling has been the dominant improvement driver across this run; with one step left, test 8192 for maximum potential loss reduction in fixed-step training.","secondary_modes":["topk","indexing"]}
